brief_justification: Paper A is a functional-analysis proof of sharp optimal constants in higher-order Hardy inequalities on the lattice Z^d, using Fourier reduction to the torus, a ground-state representation, and iterated weighted Bochner identities. Paper B is a simulation-based statistical inference of cosmic-string tension from LISA gravitational-wave data amid astrophysical foregrounds. The machinery is entirely different (deterministic inequality optimization and asymptotic constant estimation vs. Bayesian/likelihood-free parameter inference), so no role-for-role mapping holds beyond the generic notion of 'estimating a number'.
